comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
# Implementation Checklist + Test Matrix

## Checklist
- [ ] Replace tab-based console shell with 3-column layout.
- [ ] Keep top controls row always visible (scorecard/score + actions).
- [ ] Add right-pane artifact controller with `none/activity/board/report`.
- [ ] Add `Show Activity` UI action in Actions menu to open activity artifact.
- [ ] Render `ActivityDashboard` embedded in right pane.
- [ ] Keep chat/session panes visible while artifact pane is open.
- [ ] Build session list and selection model in left column.
- [ ] Wire middle pane to selected chat session.
- [ ] Add Tactus REPL + HITL loop integration boundary.
- [ ] Introduce Vercel AI Elements chat UI with adapter layer.
- [ ] Remove dark-mode light-border artifacts from Console placeholders.

## Test Matrix
- [ ] `/lab/console` shows top controls + 3-column layout.
- [ ] Right artifact pane is collapsed by default.
- [ ] No tab-selector UI remains as primary console navigation.
- [ ] `Show Activity` opens right pane to activity artifact.
- [ ] Opening activity artifact does not replace chat pane.
- [ ] Artifact pane collapse/reopen preserves artifact state.
- [ ] Embedded activity dashboard renders and respects top filters.
- [ ] Session list loads and selecting a session updates chat pane.
- [ ] Tactus/HITL REPL loop alternates assistant and HITL turns correctly.
- [ ] Dark mode shows no unintended light borders/outlines in console placeholders.

comments.text:
Implemented Console redesign pass on `feature/analysis-console-shell`:

- Replaced tab shell with chat-first 3-column console layout on `/lab/console`.
- Kept top controls always visible (`ScorecardContext` + `Actions`) and added console procedure selector.
- Added artifact pane controller (`none|activity|board|report`, open/collapse, persisted width).
- Added `Show Activity`, `Show Board`, `Show Report`, and `Run Console REPL` UI actions in the actions menu.
- Embedded `ActivityDashboard` in the right artifact pane (with shared scorecard/score state and header suppressed).
- Reused `ConversationViewer` for session list + chat pane with HITL enabled and exposed `defaultSidebarCollapsed` for console usage.
- Extended task-dispatch types/button to support non-dispatch UI actions.
- Added console adapter boundary component for chat UI composition.
- Updated `console-dashboard` tests to match the new architecture.

comments.text:
Adjusted Console artifact-pane resize direction to match expected UX:
- Dragging the divider right now narrows the right artifact pane.
- Dragging left widens the right artifact pane.

Implementation detail:
- In dashboard/components/console-dashboard.tsx, resize now computes width from rendered artifactPaneWidth and applies nextWidth = startWidth - (currentX - startX) with existing min/max clamping.

Validation note:
- Attempted to run targeted dashboard Jest test file, but local Node runtime is currently broken due missing ICU dylib (libicui18n.74.dylib).

comments.author: ryan

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Applied a second resize-direction fix for the Console artifact divider.

Change:
- Replaced delta-only drag math with right-edge anchored geometry in dashboard/components/console-dashboard.tsx.
- Width now computes from workspaceRect.right - pointerX (plus an anchor offset to avoid a drag-start jump), then clamps to min/max.
- This makes drag semantics explicit: moving pointer right reduces right-pane width; moving left increases it.
- Added global col-resize cursor during drag for consistency with other dashboard splitters.

Validation:
- Automated tests are still blocked in this environment due Node ICU dylib mismatch (libicui18n.74.dylib missing).

comments.text: Investigating console width drift during artifact resize. Plan: enforce fixed-width workspace flex constraints so resize only reallocates pane widths and never changes overall row width.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented a layout-width stabilization pass for Console pane resizing.

What changed:
- Enforced fixed workspace bounds with w-full + max-w-full and left pane w-0 + min-w-0 + flex-1 so internal panes redistribute width instead of expanding row width.
- Removed CSS max-w-[60%] constraint from the artifact pane (it could diverge from the JS width model).
- Made artifact pane flex-shrink-0 with explicit width/minWidth/maxWidth driven by the same resize state.
- Resize drag now anchors to actual rendered artifact width and current workspace right-edge geometry, with user-select disabled during drag.

Expected result:
- Total three-column row width stays fixed and fills container.
- Resizing only reallocates width between chat area and artifact pane.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Progress update: Implemented console chat stability fixes. ConversationViewer now preserves the current session selection when new sessions are created by subscription updates, adds an explicit New Session control in the session sidebar, and auto-creates/selects a fresh ACTIVE session when user sends from a COMPLETED or ERROR session. Prompt send still uses the existing HITL response path when applicable and otherwise enqueues a procedure run with console_chat metadata. Console procedure auto-selection now prioritizes procedures with recent Procedure Run task metadata indicating WAITING_FOR_HUMAN or resume metadata before fallback heuristics. Validation used direct node invocations in dashboard/: tsc --noEmit passed, console-dashboard Jest test passed, targeted eslint had no new errors (only existing hook-deps warnings in ConversationViewer).

Will include targeted frontend/backend tests and env setup docs.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented pinned Console procedure dispatch and removed fallback behavior paths for /lab/console. Frontend: Console now requires NEXT_PUBLIC_CONSOLE_PROCEDURE_ID, validates account availability, and shows explicit disabled state when missing/invalid; ConversationViewer now supports forceProcedureIdForDispatch and Console adapter forces all send dispatch/new-session flows to that procedure. Backend runtime: ProcedureChatRecorder now recognizes console_chat task metadata as a console-trigger signal and creates new sessions with category=Console when no reusable session_id exists; session reuse from console_chat.session_id remains intact. Added tests for missing/invalid pinned procedure UI states and recorder console-category creation behavior. Validation run: pytest -q plexus/cli/procedure/test_chat_recorder_account_resolution.py (6 passed), jest console-dashboard tests (4 passed) using ~/.nvm node v20.17.0, eslint on changed dashboard files (warnings only, no errors).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress update: identified the immediate no-response root cause as dispatcher env drift. The long-running dev:dispatcher process had PLEXUS_DISPATCH_MODE but was missing OPENAI_API_KEY and Plexus API env vars, so queued procedure-run tasks failed with Command failed before chat responses could be produced.

Implemented in this pass:
1) Added scripts/upsert_console_chat_procedure.py to create/update a dedicated Console procedure from plexus/procedures/console_chat_agent.yaml.
2) Added scripts/console_chat_smoke.py to automate session+message+task smoke validation.
3) Added/iterated plexus/procedures/console_chat_agent.yaml and successfully ran it directly via `plexus procedure run ce1eea23-a597-4fd3-8672-d7ce18088b11 -o json` to success:true.
4) Updated dashboard/package.json dev:dispatcher script to source ../.env and dashboard/.env.local and hard-fail if required vars are missing (PLEXUS_API_URL, PLEXUS_API_KEY, PLEXUS_ACCOUNT_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY).

Validation:
- python -m pytest plexus/cli/procedure/test_chat_recorder_account_resolution.py -q -> 7 passed
- cd dashboard && npm test -- --runInBand components/__tests__/console-dashboard.test.tsx -> passed

Operational note: existing already-running dispatcher process must be restarted to pick up the new env-loading behavior and avoid continued command-failed task handling.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented and validated console chat runtime compatibility fixes and local direct-run trigger path.

Root causes found:
1) Tactus runtime in this environment does not accept trace_sink (caused command failure).
2) With legacy runtime signatures, chat end-session call style differs (end_session(session_id, status=...)).
3) Queue-path tasks are often claimed by remote worker ip-172-31-40-145 before local processing, causing command-failed outcomes on this branch state.

Code changes:
- plexus/cli/procedure/procedure_executor.py
  - Runtime ctor is now signature-aware; passes only supported kwargs.
  - Supports both trace_sink and chat_recorder signatures.
  - Legacy chat runtime fallback now synthesizes one assistant message from procedure output when trace_sink is unavailable.
- plexus/cli/procedure/chat_recorder.py
  - end_session now supports both legacy positional status calls and Tactus-style end_session(session_id, status=...).
- dashboard/components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx
  - Chat/HITL run enqueue now creates local-direct tasks (RUNNING/DISPATCHED) and calls new local run endpoint.
  - If local trigger fails, task falls back to PENDING/PENDING queue mode.
- dashboard/app/api/console/procedure-run/route.ts
  - New node route that launches plexus procedure run with PLEXUS_DISPATCH_TASK_ID.

Tests/verification run:
- python -m pytest -q plexus/cli/procedure/test_procedure_executor_compat.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_chat_recorder_account_resolution.py (pass)
- dashboard npm test -- --runInBand components/__tests__/console-dashboard.test.tsx (pass)
- dashboard npm run typecheck (pass)
- local E2E direct binding script confirms assistant message is written into the same session after run.
- API sanity: POST /api/console/procedure-run returns accepted=true in dev server.

Operational note: queue-only path can still be pre-empted by remote worker; new local direct-run trigger is added specifically to avoid that during console interaction on this machine.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented Console response-quality/runtime fixes and validated on pinned procedure ce1eea23-a597-4fd3-8672-d7ce18088b11.

Root causes addressed:
- Console procedure was using unsupported Session.* primitives in this runtime.
- Runtime did not reliably provide the trigger user prompt to Lua procedure code.
- Trace/message fallback could persist placeholder assistant completion content.

Changes:
1) plexus/procedures/console_chat_agent.yaml (now v1.1.8)
- Replaced Session-based prompt lookup with MessageHistory-safe logic.
- Added input.console_user_message and used it as highest-priority user prompt source.
- Kept robust extraction/fallback for assistant output and removed noisy JSON-encoding path.
2) plexus/cli/procedure/chat_recorder.py
- Added get_latest_console_trigger_message().
- _get_latest_console_chat_metadata() now prefers PLEXUS_DISPATCH_TASK_ID task metadata first, preventing drift to unrelated recent tasks.
3) plexus/cli/procedure/procedure_executor.py
- Injects console_user_message into runtime context when available.
- Hardened call via getattr(...) so legacy test doubles/mocks without the new recorder method continue to work.
- Improved assistant-message synthesis policy so result.response is recorded when traces are absent/non-meaningful.
4) plexus/cli/procedure/tactus_adapters/trace.py
- Drops placeholder assistant trace content (Assistant turn completed.) and tracks meaningful assistant texts.
5) Tests added/updated:
- test_procedure_executor_compat.py: context injection + synthesis/duplication cases.
- test_trace_sink.py: placeholder-drop behavior.
- test_chat_recorder_account_resolution.py: dispatch-task metadata/session reuse + trigger message retrieval.

Verification:
- python -m pytest -q plexus/cli/procedure/test_procedure_executor_compat.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_trace_sink.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_chat_recorder_account_resolution.py -> 25 passed.
- Procedure republished via scripts/upsert_console_chat_procedure.py to pinned ID above.
- Direct run bound to dispatch task (PLEXUS_DISPATCH_TASK_ID=... plexus procedure run ... -o json) now shows prompt_used equal to the actual user message and returns a meaningful assistant response (example: Hello! How can I assist you today?).

Operational note: queued tasks created as PENDING are often claimed by remote worker ip-172-31-40-145... and fail with generic Command failed before local validation. Local-direct task path remains the reliable route for immediate console testing on this machine.

comments.text: Investigated current continuity/multi-reply failure with live data. Root cause confirmed: console run tasks are still created with dispatchStatus=PENDING, so local task dispatcher can claim them and run the same task path in parallel with the SQS console worker. Evidence: recent task metadata shows dispatch_mode flipped to local for a console-triggered task, and corresponding session shows duplicate assistant turns. Next patch: make startConsoleRun create tasks as dispatchStatus=DISPATCHED (already queued), and add dispatcher-side guard to skip console_async_worker tasks even if they appear pending. Then harden builtin console prompt continuity by explicitly carrying prior user turn context into assistant instructions and add regression tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Patched and verified the multi-reply/continuity regression path.

Implemented:
1) startConsoleRun now creates tasks with dispatchStatus=DISPATCHED (status stays PENDING). This marks the run as already dispatched by SQS and prevents generic dispatchers from claiming it.
2) Generic CLI dispatcher now explicitly skips tasks whose metadata.dispatch_mode == console_async_worker, preventing duplicate local execution if such tasks appear pending.
3) Built-in console TAC prompt now includes explicit prior-user-turn context and a stronger continuity rule; added guard that rewrites fallback forgetful replies when prior user context is available.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Worker correctness + continuity patch in progress:
- Fixed console worker execution path to run ProcedureService directly instead of experiment_runner (removes statemachine import-chain failure seen in staging tasks: ModuleNotFoundError: statemachine).
- Worker now maps run result status to task status (COMPLETED/WAITING_FOR_HUMAN/FAILED) and persists output on task update.
- Hardened startConsoleRun history normalization/merge: skip non-MESSAGE entries and merge server/client history by suffix-prefix overlap to avoid duplicated A,B,A,B snapshots.
- Filtered fallback DB history in chat_recorder to exclude ASSISTANT INTERNAL messages from continuity context.
Validation run locally: py_compile ok, amplify resolver TS compile ok, chat_recorder tests pass.
Next: deploy to staging, rerun automated 2-turn continuity smoke, then validate no duplicate assistant turns and reference-following works ("Multiply that by three" style).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Found root cause for continuity failures: startConsoleRun currently prefers clientInstrumentation.client_user_message_text over the authoritative trigger ChatMessage content. If the client-side instrumentation is stale, task metadata trigger_message_content is wrong, history tail is rewritten incorrectly, and follow-up turns lose context. Implementing resolver fix now to prefer trigger ChatMessage content, only fallback to instrumentation when trigger content is empty, then re-running 2-turn smoke.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented fix in dashboard/amplify/data/resolvers/startConsoleRun.ts: trigger_message_content now prefers the authoritative trigger ChatMessage.content and only falls back to client instrumentation when content is empty. This prevents stale client_user_message_text from overriding turn input and corrupting merged history tails. Validation: amplify resolver TS compile passes (npx tsc --noEmit -p dashboard/amplify/tsconfig.json), python compile passes for worker/chat recorder, and chat recorder test suite still green.
